author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Landed the v3 git-only write path on branch `v3-write-path` in
`~/p/jjforge-v3-write` (commit `74827e93`).

- `crates/jjf-storage/src/git.rs` — typed wrapper over the `git`
  CLI: `hash_object`, `mktree`, `commit_tree`, `update_ref`,
  `cat_blob`, `resolve_ref`, `for_each_ref`, plus a CAS-failure
  detector mirroring `JjError::is_concurrent_write`.
- `crates/jjf-storage/src/v3_write.rs` — issue + memory commit
  builders. Trees carry `issue.json` (always) and
  `comments.jsonl` (only when an issue has comments).
- `Storage` carries a new `mode: StorageMode` discriminator. At
  `open` time we probe `refs/jjf/meta/format-version`; presence
  routes writes through the git-only path, absence keeps the v2
  4-CLI dance. v2 reads / writes are unchanged.
- All five mutating chokepoints dispatch on `mode`: `create_issue`,
  `mutate_once` (covers every scalar setter), `add_comment_once`,
  `set_memory`, `unset_memory`. `find_open_slug_collision` also
  has a v3 path (walks per-issue refs since the snapshot cache is
  v2-shape; the read-path ticket ports it).
- The existing concurrent-write retry policy works unchanged on
  v3 — CAS failures inside `v3_write` translate to
  `Error::ConcurrentWrite` before they reach `mutate`'s match arm.

# Recommendation

Close. Every acceptance box ticks:
- New git.rs module, four+ helpers landed.
- v3 dispatch wired into every mutating method.
- v2 dance preserved for v2-shape repos (verified: full pre-
  existing test suite still green).
- Concurrent-write retry exercised on v3 by a new test that races
  a stale-tip mutate.
- No `jj` subprocess on the v3 write path — enforced by a grep
  test that scans every non-comment line of `git.rs` and
  `v3_write.rs` for `Command::new("jj")` and `JjRepo`.
- New integration tests cover create / mutate / add_comment /
  memory set+unset, and assert git HEAD's oid + symbolic target
  are unchanged plus the jj `@` change is unchanged across every
  mutation.
- `cargo nextest run --workspace`: 431 passed, 0 failed.

# Confidence

High on the write-path mechanics — every code path landed in a
fresh test fixture and the v2 suite stays green. Medium on the
"the migrator will reuse this cleanly" axis: the v3 write API
takes typed inputs (`IssueRecord`, `&[Comment]`, `&Memory`) so
the migrator can drive it directly without re-routing through
the public mutators; verifying that under load is ticket
`c14e1c1`'s problem.

# Open follow-ups

- The read path (ticket `6e2c843`) still goes through the v2
  bookmark / snapshot cache. On a v3-shape test fixture
  `Storage::read` / `list_ready` won't work until that ticket
  ports them. The v3 write-path tests work around this by going
  directly through git CLI to verify the ref state.
- `find_open_slug_collision_v3` walks every issue ref linearly —
  fine for the planner's N, will need a v3-aware snapshot cache
  for asterinas-scale N (ticket `b3c2cda` (cache port) covers it).
- The format-version sentinel's planter (`write_format_version_sentinel`)
  is implemented but not wired into `Storage::init`. Ticket
  `add0646` (init rewrite) wires it.
- `record_merge*` (the v2 multi-head merge driver) stays v2-only.
  v3 divergence is per-issue and is handled by the push/pull
  refspec ticket (`d4f1ae0`).
